extracted.brandBackground: Urban Air Adventure Park operates indoor adventure parks featuring trampolines, ropes courses, and other recreational attractions.

leadership.name: Mandy Dowson
leadership.background: Appointed Brand President in January 2026, previously serving as CEO of Jenny Craig.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs the overall strategic vision and brand execution for the franchise system.

leadership.name: Tim Sharp
leadership.background: Served as President from December 2024 to December 2025 and VP of Operations from 2019 to 2024.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages ongoing franchisee relations and operational support systems.

leadership.name: Eden Bullock
leadership.background: Appointed VP of Operations in January 2026, previously a leadership consultant at TopTalent Learning.
leadership.whyItMatters: Oversees daily park operations, training programs, and brand standards compliance.

leadership.name: Michael Browning, Jr.
leadership.role: Chief Executive Officer of Unleashed Services
leadership.background: Co-founder of UATP and CEO of Unleashed Brands since July 2021.
leadership.whyItMatters: Leads the parent platform that provides executive management to all affiliated brands.

leadership.name: Stephen Polozola
leadership.role: Chief Legal Officer of Unleashed Services
leadership.background: Co-founder of UATP and General Counsel since inception in May 2013.
leadership.whyItMatters: Directs legal strategy, franchise compliance, and intellectual property protection.

leadership.name: Jon Shell
leadership.role: Chief Financial Officer of Unleashed Services
leadership.background: Appointed CFO in December 2024, previously serving as CEO and CFO of Neighborly.
leadership.whyItMatters: Manages financial planning, reporting, and capital allocation across the portfolio.

item19.notes: Excludes 1.0 and 1.5 Trampoline Parks as they are no longer offered, as well as units that opened or closed during 2025 or did not provide complete data.

Typically equal to one month's base rent.

flatMonthlyFees.name: POS System Fee and Computer Systems

hiddenCosts.name: U.S. Tariff on Chinese Imports
hiddenCosts.description: A 145% tariff on Chinese imports directly impacts the cost of purchasing required base and optional attractions.

hiddenCosts.name: Technology Fee
hiddenCosts.description: Up to 0.25% of Gross Sales for basic troubleshooting support if implemented.

operationalRisks.title: Out-of-State Dispute Resolution
operationalRisks.description: Disputes must be resolved by arbitration or litigation in Tarrant County, Texas, which may increase costs.

operationalRisks.title: Parent Platform Litigation History
operationalRisks.description: Parent company Unleashed Brands has faced multiple legal proceedings from franchisees questioning operational changes.

extracted.conceptRationale: The concept is an indoor adventure park featuring active entertainment attractions alongside an on-site café.
extracted.staffingRationale: Requires a full-time general manager and a large team of on-site staff to operate attractions, front desk, and café.

riskReasons: Capital gap of $3,897,223 is ~16x your liquid — extreme leverage; the deal rests almost entirely on outside capital.

underwriting.assessment: With $250,000 liquid against a $4,147,223 mid-point build-out, there is a $3,897,223 capital gap. An SBA loan or an equity partner is required to do this deal. At mid-cohort revenue, modeled cash flow after debt service is about $175,492/mo before payroll and maintenance — a thin margin of error.

insights.conceptLabel: Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)
insights.conceptRationale: The concept is an indoor adventure park featuring active entertainment attractions alongside an on-site café.

benchmark.label: Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)
benchmark.dominantRisk: Blended margin. The bar/kitchen attach rate swings COGS up; the large footprint plus simulator/equipment R&M swing fixed costs. Owner-operator vs. absentee is a full GM salary either way.
benchmark.rampNote: 6–18 months to a mature membership/booking base; the Item 19 average is NOT a year-one number.
considerations: Budget blended COGS ~15–28% (sim time and memberships carry little; the bar and kitchen drive it) and labor ~22–30%.
Decide owner-operator vs. absentee before modeling — absentee adds a GM salary (~$60–90K/yr) the pro forma omits.
Confirm the F&B attach rate and per-bay equipment costs (e.g. simulator licenses, R&M) at mature franchised units via Item 20.
Assume a 6–18 month ramp; discount the Item 19 mature average for year one.

crossCheck.message: No operating-EBITDA margin is disclosed in Item 19. The 15–26% band below is an industry estimate for this concept — validate it against real franchisee P&Ls (Item 20) before relying on any margin.

buildup.label: − Labor
buildup.note: conventionally staffed · ≈ 16.3–22.3 FTE at ~$20/hr fully loaded

assumptions.detail: Classified as “Experiential entertainment with F&B attach (e.g. indoor golf + bar)” from the FDD (Item 1 / brand description).
Taken from the FDD (~$25,000/mo); already netted in margin after fees & rent.
Estimated at the 15–28% category band — COGS is never disclosed in an FDD.
Estimated at the 22–30% conventionally staffed band — labor is never disclosed in an FDD.
Utilities, insurance, and R&M estimated at 6–10% (category catch-all).
Our calculation — the disclosed top line minus the benchmarked costs above. Not a figure stated in the FDD.

insights.consultCtaPitch: These are category benchmarks applied to the disclosed top line — your unit's real costs will differ, and that difference is the conversation. Want them pressure-tested against actual franchisee P&Ls for your territory?
insights.asOf: 2026 — general industry benchmarks, refine against real franchisee P&Ls
insights.disclaimer: Industry benchmark ranges for your own budgeting and Item 20 questions — NOT a projection of this franchise's results, and not investment advice.

financialCondition.headline: The franchisor's statements don't show the distress signals we screen for — positive net worth and a clean audit. Still worth a quick confirmation against current figures.
body: Why it matters to you: field support, training, technology, and marketing come out of the franchisor's budget. If outside funding slows, those are the first things to get cut. UA Holdings, LLC guarantees the franchisor's performance, which partly backstops this.

Verify before you commit: ask for the most recent interim (unaudited) financials and the current-year funding plan; ask whether the parent will sign a guarantee of performance; and cross-check Item 20 — unit closures, transfers, and terminations are the operational tell that corroborates financial stress.
mitigants: the auditor issued a clean (unmodified) opinion and did not raise going-concern doubt
the parent guarantees the franchisor's performance
financialCondition.evidenceNote: Derived from the Item 21 / Exhibit F audited financials. Figures are extracted; all ratios and grading are computed. Confirm against the source statements and a CPA before relying on them.
